ogTitle: Frazers Creek vs Freeburn Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Frazers Creek and Freeburn Island.
heading: Frazers Creek vs Freeburn Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Frazers Creek, New South Wales and Freeburn Island, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Frazers Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Freeburn Island (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Frazers Creek or Freeburn Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Frazers Creek has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Freeburn Island scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Frazers Creek scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Freeburn Island scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Frazers Creek or Freeburn Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Frazers Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
